sanskrit:
मामुपेत्य पुनर्जन्म दुःखालयमशाश्वतम्।
नाप्नुवन्ति महात्मानः संसिद्धिं परमां गताः॥
transliteration:
mām upetya punar janma duḥkhālayam aśāśvatam
nāpnuvanti mahātmānaḥ saṁsiddhiṁ paramāṁ gatāḥ
literal_meaning: Having attained Me, the great souls no longer return to the temporary abode of suffering.
contextual_meaning: When the mind fully internalizes a stable, unconditional source of meaning (symbolized by Kṛṣṇa), it stops recycling the same painful life narratives; the ‘return’ is psychological recurrence, not physical rebirth.
psychological_state: Post-attachment; the nervous system no longer scans for the next problem to solve because the reward circuitry has re-centered on an internalized, non-contingent object.
trigger_scenario: The recovering addict who, after years of white-knuckling sobriety, suddenly realizes the craving story has gone quiet—there is no ‘next drink’ to resist.
anchor_text: Rest in the changeless; the mind stops rewriting its pain.
